channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Russell puts Mercedes on pole at Las Vegas and Verstappen nips Norris in championship battle
item.description: George Russell put Mercedes on the pole for the Las Vegas Grand Prix in an upset over teammate Lewis Hamilton.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jai'Den Thomas leads No. 23 UNLV to a 27-16 victory over San Jose State
item.description: Jai’Den Thomas ran for 135 yards and the go-ahead touchdown late in the third quarter to help No. 23 UNLV keep its conference title hopes alive with a 27-16 victory over San Jose State.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alyssa Nakken, first full-time female coach in MLB history, leaving Giants to join Guardians
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Alyssa Nakken, the first woman to coach in a Major League Baseball game, is leaving the San Francisco Giants to join the Cleveland Guardians. Nakken made history in 2022 when she took over as first-base coach following an ejection.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: F1 legend Mario Andretti talks American drivers, constructors getting back onto grid ahead of Las Vegas GP
item.description: Formula 1 legend Mario Andretti talked to Fox News Digital about the popularity of the sport in the United States and whether another American driver or team could help boost it even more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Royals acquire second baseman Jonathan India from Reds for right-hander Brady Singer
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Kansas City Royals acquired second baseman Jonathan India from the Cincinnati Reds on Friday, adding a dependable leadoff hitter and middle infielder to play alongside star shortstop Bobby Witt Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caitlin Clark investing in team ownership bid despite 5-figure WNBA salary and turning down bigger offer
item.description: Caitlin Clark of the Indiana Fever is investing in a bid for a potential National Women's Soccer League expansion team based in Cincinnati.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 76ers center Joel Embiid sidelined due to swelling in his left knee and will miss two games
item.description: The Philadelphia 76ers say center Joel Embiid is managing swelling in his left knee and will miss a second consecutive game Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brock Purdy will miss Sunday's game for the 49ers with a shoulder injury
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — San Francisco quarterback Brock Purdy will miss Sunday's game against the Green Bay Packers with a sore throwing shoulder, dealing another blow to the 49ers' playoff hopes. Purdy injured his right shoulder in last Sunday's loss to the Seattle Seahawks.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giants exec slams ESPN's Elle Duncan after mocking Daniel Jones for reading statement prior to release
item.description: New York Giants senior vice president of communications Pat Hanlon took exception to ESPN's Elle Duncan's segment during Thursday's "SportsCenter" that mocked Daniel Jones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caitlin Clark to join Cincinnati bid for 16th National Women's Soccer League team
item.description: WNBA star Caitlin Clark has joined Cincinnati's bid for a National Women's Soccer League expansion team. Major League Soccer's FC Cincinnati heads the group trying to bring a women's pro team to the city.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Martin Slumbers hangs his hat on making British Open big and promoting women's golf
item.description: Martin Slumbers could not have witnessed greater theater than his first British Open in charge of the R&A, the record-setting duel between Henrik Stenson and Phil Mickelson at Royal Troon. His only concern in 2016 was the audience.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz blow 20-point lead to Spurs, lose fourth straight
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (AP) - Harrison Barnes had 25 points and 10 rebounds, rookie Stephon Castle scored 18 points and the San Antonio Spurs rallied to defeat the Utah Jazz 126-118 on Thursday night for their second straight win without Victor Wembanyama.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: MLB will test robot umpires at 13 spring training ballparks hosting 19 teams
item.description: MLB has been experimenting with the automated ball-strike system in the minor leagues since 2019 but is still working on the shape of the strike zone.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Connor Bedard trying to break out of slump as the Blackhawks struggle to score
item.description: Bedard was terrific last season, living up to the considerable hype that has followed him around for years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bears vs. Packers: Injuries, inactives, key matchups and more
item.description: CHICAGO — It's been a tumultuous several weeks for the Bears. It seems like forever since they went into the bye week feeling good with a 4-2 record. Since then, there's been a shocking Hail Mary loss in Washington and lifeless defeats to Arizona and New England.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Utes get run over by #17 Colorado, 49-24
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes losing streak continues, with no end in sight. Shedeur Sanders overcame a shaky start to throw three touchdown passes, LaJohntay Wester returned a punt 76 yards for a score and No. 18 Colorado pulled away late to beat Utah 49-24 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Draymond Green's foul against Zach Edey is upgraded by the NBA to a flagrant 1
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green's foul against Memphis' Zach Edey in the third quarter of the Warriors' victory over the Grizzlies has been upgraded to a category-one flagrant, the NBA said Saturday after reviewing the play.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
